data.title: Brevo Review: Why I Recommend It for Growing Businesses
data.excerpt: Discover why Brevo stands out as an affordable all-in-one CRM and marketing platform for email, SMS, WhatsApp, and automation. Try it free today.

$media.alt: Brevo Review: Why I Recommend It for Growing Businesses

children.text: If you've been searching for a marketing platform that actually delivers without draining your budget, let me save you some time:
Brevo

deserves a spot at the top of your list. I've tested dozens of email marketing tools over the years, and Brevo consistently impresses me with its combination of power, simplicity, and pricing that makes sense for real businesses—not just enterprise teams with bottomless budgets.

I want to be upfront: this post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through my links, I may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. That said, every recommendation here comes from genuine experience with the platform.
Let me walk you through exactly why Brevo has earned my trust and why it might be the perfect fit for your business.
What Makes Brevo Different from Every Other Email Tool

Most email marketing platforms follow the same playbook: charge you based on how many contacts you have. The moment your list grows past a few thousand subscribers, your monthly bill explodes. Brevo flipped that model on its head.

With Brevo, pricing is based on the number of emails you send, not the number of contacts you store. This is a massive deal. You could have 50,000 contacts in your CRM and still pay a reasonable monthly fee, as long as you're sending a manageable volume of emails. For businesses that are actively growing their audience—which should be every business—this pricing structure removes one of the biggest friction points in email marketing.

But pricing alone doesn't make a platform worth recommending. What sealed the deal for me is that Brevo isn't just an email tool. It's a full multichannel marketing and CRM platform rolled into one dashboard. We're talking email campaigns, SMS marketing, WhatsApp messaging, live chat, and even transactional emails—all managed from a single interface. No more juggling five different subscriptions and praying your integrations don't break.
If you're ready to experience this yourself,
sign up for Brevo's free plan here
—no credit card needed.
Key Features That Make Brevo Shine
Let's get specific about what you actually get when you use Brevo. Here's the stuff that matters most in day-to-day use.
Drag-and-Drop Email Editor

Building emails in Brevo feels intuitive from minute one. The drag-and-drop editor lets you pull in text blocks, images, buttons, dividers, and social links without touching a single line of code. You can create polished, professional-looking emails in under 15 minutes—even if your design skills are limited to choosing between Comic Sans and Papyrus (please don't use either).

The responsive templates are genuinely useful, too. They're organized by industry and use case, so whether you're running an e-commerce store, a SaaS company, or a local service business, you'll find starting points that actually look good on mobile.
AI Content Generator

This one surprised me. Brevo includes an AI-powered content assistant that helps you draft subject lines, write email copy, and adjust the tone of your messages. It's not a gimmick—it's a practical tool that saves real time, especially when you're running multiple campaigns per week and staring at a blank screen.
Advanced Segmentation

Segmentation is where Brevo truly flexes. You can filter contacts by demographics, website activity, campaign engagement, purchase history, and custom events. Create as many segments as you need, save them, and use them across campaigns. This level of granularity usually requires a much more expensive platform.
SMS and WhatsApp Marketing

Email is powerful, but it's not the only channel your customers check. Brevo lets you send SMS campaigns and WhatsApp messages right from the same dashboard where you manage your email lists. You can even mix channels in a single automation workflow—send an email, follow up with an SMS if it's not opened, and trigger a WhatsApp message for high-intent leads.
Built-in CRM

Brevo includes a CRM that's clean and functional. You can track deals, manage your sales pipeline, and see every interaction a contact has had with your brand—emails opened, pages visited, forms submitted—in one timeline view. It's not trying to replace Salesforce, and that's exactly the point. It's the right-sized CRM for growing teams that need visibility without complexity.
Marketing Automation

The automation builder in Brevo uses a visual workflow editor where you set triggers, conditions, delays, and actions. Welcome sequences, abandoned cart reminders, post-purchase follow-ups, re-engagement campaigns—you can build all of them without needing a developer. The automation capabilities rival tools that cost three to five times more.
Want to see how these features work together in practice? Check out
our guide on how to use Brevo step by step
New and Highlighted Features You Should Know About
Brevo keeps adding functionality at a pace that genuinely impresses me. Here are some of the most notable recent additions:
AI Content Generator

— I mentioned this above, but it's worth calling out as a standout feature. The AI assistant helps you generate subject lines and body copy, rewrite text, and match the tone to your brand voice. It's integrated directly into the email editor, so there's no need to switch between tabs or copy-paste from ChatGPT.
Landing Pages
— You can now build landing pages directly inside Brevo. Pair them with your forms and automations to create complete lead generation funnels without a separate tool like Leadpages or Unbounce.
Transactional Email Improvements

— Brevo has always been strong on transactional emails (order confirmations, password resets, shipping notifications), but recent updates have made deliverability even more reliable. If you run an e-commerce store, this alone is worth the switch from a basic email provider.
Conversations (Live Chat + Chatbot)

— The built-in live chat widget lets you talk to website visitors in real time and set up chatbot flows for after-hours support. It connects directly to your CRM, so every chat becomes a trackable contact record.
Meeting Scheduling
— Brevo now includes a scheduling feature so prospects can book meetings directly from your emails or landing pages. No need for a separate Calendly subscription.
Want the full breakdown of what's new? Read our
detailed guide to Brevo's latest features
Pricing That Actually Makes Sense
Let's talk numbers, because this is where Brevo really separates itself from the competition.
Free Plan

— You get 300 emails per day (roughly 9,000 per month), unlimited contacts, the email editor, templates, SMS marketing, and email support. For a solopreneur or someone just getting started, this is remarkably generous.
Starter Plan

— Starting at just $9 per month (or $8.08 per month billed annually with a 10% discount), you unlock 5,000 emails per month, remove Brevo branding, and get access to email and SMS features with the drag-and-drop editor and advanced segmentation.
Business Plan
— This adds marketing automation, A/B testing, advanced statistics, multi-user access, and phone support. It's where most growing businesses will want to land.
Enterprise
— Custom pricing for large teams with dedicated account management and priority support.

The key takeaway: you're never penalized for growing your contact list. As your audience expands, you only need to adjust your sending volume. This is the pricing model every email platform should adopt.
Ready to test it without spending a dime?
Create your free Brevo account
and start building your first campaign today.
For practical strategies on maximizing your budget, take a look at our
Brevo tips and best practices
Who Is Brevo Ideal For?
Brevo works beautifully across a wide range of users, but here's where I see it delivering the most value:
Small Business Owners

— If you need email marketing, a CRM, and maybe SMS campaigns, but you don't want to pay for three separate tools, Brevo consolidates everything into one affordable platform. The learning curve is gentle enough that you can be running campaigns within your first hour.
E-commerce Stores

— Between transactional emails, abandoned cart sequences, product launch campaigns, and order follow-ups, e-commerce businesses live and die by their email infrastructure. Brevo handles all of this reliably, and the contact-based pricing means your bill stays predictable even during peak growth seasons.
Agencies and Freelancers
— If you manage marketing for multiple clients, Brevo's multi-user support and agency-friendly features make it easy to keep client accounts organized without drowning in logins and dashboards.
Content Creators and Newsletter Operators

— Running a newsletter with 10,000+ subscribers on a traditional email platform can get expensive fast. Brevo's sending-based pricing means you can grow your audience aggressively without worrying about your costs scaling linearly with every new subscriber.
Startups and SaaS Companies

— The combination of marketing automation, transactional email, live chat, and CRM in one platform is a game-changer for lean startup teams. You get enterprise-level capabilities at a fraction of the cost.
Nonprofits and Community Organizations
— With a generous free plan and affordable paid tiers, Brevo makes professional-grade email marketing accessible even to organizations with tight budgets.
Looking for real-world inspiration? Browse our collection of
Brevo use cases
to see how different businesses are using the platform.
How Brevo Stacks Up Against the Competition
I'm not here to trash-talk other platforms—there are plenty of solid tools out there. But here's where Brevo genuinely shines in comparison:
vs. Mailchimp

— Mailchimp charges based on contacts, which gets expensive quickly. Brevo charges based on sending volume, which is far more scalable. Brevo also includes a built-in CRM and SMS marketing, features that Mailchimp either lacks or charges extra for.
vs. ActiveCampaign

— ActiveCampaign has powerful automation, but it comes with a steeper learning curve and a higher price tag. Brevo offers comparable automation capabilities with a more approachable interface and significantly lower entry cost.
vs. Constant Contact
— Constant Contact is fine for basic newsletters, but Brevo offers far more channels (WhatsApp, live chat, transactional email) and deeper segmentation at a similar or lower price point.
vs. HubSpot

— HubSpot's free CRM is excellent, but the moment you need marketing automation or email at scale, you're looking at hundreds of dollars per month. Brevo gives you CRM, email, SMS, automation, and chat at a fraction of that cost.
The pattern is consistent: Brevo delivers more features per dollar than almost any competitor in its class.
Getting Started Is Effortless
One thing I really appreciate about Brevo is how fast you can go from signup to sending your first campaign. Here's the typical path:
Sign up for free
— Takes about 30 seconds. No credit card required.
Import your contacts
— Upload a CSV, connect your existing tools, or add contacts manually.
Build your first email
— Use the drag-and-drop editor or pick a template.
Set up a form or landing page
— Start capturing new leads right away.
Create your first automation
— A welcome sequence is a great place to start.
Send and analyze
— Track opens, clicks, and conversions from your dashboard.
You can realistically have a complete email marketing system up and running in a single afternoon.
If you prefer visual guidance, watch this walkthrough video where Ecom Mastery explains
why Brevo is the best email marketing and CRM platform for small businesses
—it's a solid overview that covers the core workflow.
The Bottom Line

Brevo earns its place in my recommended tools list because it solves real problems without creating new ones. The pricing is fair and transparent. The feature set covers email, SMS, WhatsApp, chat, CRM, and automation—all in one place. The interface is clean enough for beginners but powerful enough for experienced marketers. And the free plan is genuinely useful, not just a teaser designed to force an upgrade.

I've seen too many businesses overpay for marketing tools that underdeliver. Brevo is the opposite: it overdelivers at every price point. Whether you're a solo creator sending a weekly newsletter or a growing e-commerce brand running complex automation workflows, Brevo has a plan that fits.
If you've made it this far, you already know this tool is worth trying.
Start your free Brevo account now
and see for yourself why thousands of businesses are making the switch. You've got nothing to lose and a whole lot of marketing power to gain.

children.text: Is Brevo really free to use?

Yes, Brevo offers a free-forever plan that includes 300 emails per day (roughly 9,000 per month), unlimited contacts, the drag-and-drop email editor, templates, SMS marketing, and email support. No credit card is required to sign up, and you can use the free plan for as long as you want.
How is Brevo's pricing different from Mailchimp?

The biggest difference is that Brevo charges based on the number of emails you send, not the number of contacts you store. This means you can grow your contact list to tens of thousands of subscribers without your bill increasing, as long as your sending volume stays the same. Mailchimp charges based on contacts, which gets expensive as your list grows.
Can I use Brevo for SMS and WhatsApp marketing?

Absolutely. Brevo supports email, SMS, and WhatsApp marketing all from the same dashboard. You can send standalone SMS or WhatsApp campaigns, or incorporate them into multi-channel automation workflows—for example, sending an email first, then an SMS reminder if the email isn't opened.
Does Brevo include a CRM?

Yes, Brevo has a built-in CRM that lets you manage contacts, track deals through your sales pipeline, and view every interaction a contact has had with your brand in one timeline. It's designed for small to mid-sized businesses that need CRM functionality without the complexity and cost of enterprise solutions like Salesforce.
